steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and show past tense meaning
Changed pattern to show who does the action
Added ة to make it a specific instance or event rather than just a doer
Added ال to make it definite and show this is a specific, known event
Added ُ to show this is the subject of the sentence
This is a question word that asks 'what?' about something. In this ayah, it's asking about ٱلْقَارِعَةُ (The Striking Calamity) that comes after it.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The middle letter 'ا' was added and the last vowel changed to 'ِ' to change from 'he struck' to 'one who strikes' - like changing 'hit' to 'hitter'

steps.m: the great catastrophe (lit: the female striker)
steps.n: Adding ة makes it refer to a specific catastrophic event (like the Day of Judgment) rather than just someone who strikes

steps.m: The Great Catastrophe (name of Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Adding ٱلْ makes it definite (like 'the' in English), and the ُ shows it's the subject. This is one of the names for the Day of Judgment, referring to how it will strike with terror.
This is a question word used to ask 'what' about something
Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this question to what came before it
The long alif (آ) at the end makes the word stronger and adds emphasis when reading

steps.m: to know deeply or be fully aware
steps.n: Basic meaning of knowing or understanding something
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added أَ at beginning with sukun on د to make the verb causative (making someone else do the action)
Added alif maksura with superscript alif for proper writing of final weak letter

steps.m: made you know (object of وَمَآ - 'and what')
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show 'you' as the object receiving the action. This word follows وَمَآ (and what) in the question 'And what made you know?'
Basic interrogative particle used to ask questions
Added fatha mark to show clear pronunciation. In this ayah, it's asking about القَارِعَةُ (the Striking Calamity)
Basic meaning of striking or knocking on something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to show who does the action
Added ة to make it feminine and intensify the meaning. In the Quran, this word specifically refers to the Day of Judgment.

steps.m: The Great Striking Calamity (referring to Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added ال to make it definite. The hamza is written as ٱ because it's at the start of the word.

steps.m: The Great Striking Calamity (referring to Day of Judgment)
steps.n: Added damma at the end because this word is the subject of the sentence
Connected the letters together and added vowel marks to make it into a noun meaning 'day'

steps.m: on the day when (object of يَكُونُ)
steps.n: Added a fatha at the end to show this word tells us when something happens. It's connected to the verb يَكُونُ (will be) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form

steps.m: he becomes/is (subject of يَوْمَ - on the Day)
steps.n: The prefix يَ shows this is happening in the future. The ُ (damma) on ك and at the end are needed because it's a present tense verb. This describes what will happen on the Day of Judgment.
Forms the basic noun for 'people' by adding alif after first letter and dropping the hamza. The meaning connects to the root since humans are social beings who are familiar with each other.
Adds 'the' (ال) at the start and doubles the ن sound

steps.m: the people (subject of يَكُونُ - will be)
steps.n: Shows this word is the subject (doer) of the verb يَكُونُ (will be) in this ayah
The ا after ر changes it from a verb (to spread) into a noun meaning something that spreads out a lot, like moths/butterflies
Added ال to make it specific
Added كَ to show comparison and ِ because it follows a preposition. This describes how people will be scattered like moths on that day.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed to show the thing that was scattered rather than the one doing the scattering
Added ال to make it specific and show it's describing the moths mentioned before

steps.m: the scattered (describing moths in a way that shows connection)
steps.n: Added kasra to show this word is connected to and describing الفراش (moths)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Added تَ at start to show present tense and changed vowels to show ongoing action. The mountains (الجبال) is the subject doing this action.
Added وَ at start to connect this with what came before
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic singular word for mountain
Changes the singular 'mountain' into plural form for many mountains
Added ال to make it specific mountains

steps.m: the mountains (subject of تَكُونُ - will become)
steps.n: Added ُ at end to show it's doing the action. This word is the subject of the verb تَكُونُ (will become) - it tells us what will become like carded wool.
Added kasra after first letter and sukun after second to form the basic noun for wool
Added ال with proper pronunciation marks to make it specific wool
Added كَ at start to mean 'like' and kasra at end because it follows the preposition كَ. The word describes how the mountains will become like fluffy wool on the Day of Judgment.

steps.m: to spread out or fluff up (like wool)

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to show the wool that has been fluffed up rather than the action of fluffing

steps.m: the spread out/fluffed up (describing the wool)
steps.n: Added ال to specify this particular wool being described

steps.m: the spread out/fluffed up (describing كَٱلْعِهْنِ/like wool)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because this word is describing كَٱلْعِهْنِ (like wool) in the ayah
This is a basic particle used to introduce a condition or topic

steps.m: then as for (introduces what happens next in مَن ثَقُلَتْ مَوٰزِينُهُۥ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from something before. In this ayah, it introduces what happens to those whose scales are heavy.
This is a special word that refers to people. In this ayah, it means 'whoever' and is connected to the verb ثَقُلَتْ (became heavy) - so it's talking about whoever's scales become heavy.
The vowels show this describes something naturally getting heavier

steps.m: it became heavy (feminine form for مَوٰزِينُهُۥ - his scales)
steps.n: Added ت to match with مَوٰزِينُهُۥ (scales) which is treated as feminine in Arabic
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the letters
Added مِ at start to make it a tool name. The و changed to ي because it comes after كسرة
Changed from single scale to plural by adding wa (و) and changing the vowels - like how we make many English words plural by adding 's'
Added ُ to show this is the doer of ثَقُلَتْ (became heavy)
Added هُ to show possession (his). The long alif is written as ٰ in this style.
Added vowel marks to show the correct pronunciation of the basic pronoun

steps.m: so he/it (subject of the phrase فِى عِيشَةٍ رَّاضِيَةٍ)
steps.n: Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before. This connects to the next phrase 'in a pleasant life'
This is a basic preposition that shows location or position. In this ayah, it connects 'he' (هُوَ) to 'life/living' (عِيشَةٍ), showing he is in that state.
The ي is written as ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a spelling change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Changed from root meaning 'to live comfortably' into a noun meaning 'comfortable living' by adding vowel marks
Changed the vowel pattern and added ة to make it refer to a specific state or instance of comfortable living

steps.m: a state of comfortable living (describing رَّاضِيَةٍ)
steps.n: Added tanween kasra to show this is describing the word رَّاضِيَةٍ (satisfying/pleasing) that follows it in the ayah
Added shadda to emphasize the intensity of the contentment. This word describes عِيشَةٍ (life) in this ayah.
The weak letter و changes to ي and we add ا after first letter to show it's describing who/what is doing the action
The ة makes this feminine to match with عِيشَة which is feminine
Adds the genitive case ending to match grammatical position

steps.m: as for (introduces what follows مَنْ خَفَّتْ)
steps.n: This is a compound particle that introduces a condition or contrast. Here it sets up what will be said about those whose scales are light.

steps.m: and as for (introduces what follows مَنْ خَفَّتْ)
steps.n: The و connects this statement about one group to a previous statement about another group, showing contrast between them
This is a basic word used to ask about or refer to people. In this ayah, it refers to people whose scales will be light.
Added sukun mark to show the end of the word. This word is connected to خَفَّتْ (became light) - it tells us who the action happened to.
The doubled letter (shadda) shows this happened strongly or completely. This word describes what happened to مَوٰزِينُهُۥ (his scales) in this ayah.

steps.m: it became very light (subject of مَوٰزِينُهُۥ)
steps.n: The ت shows this is talking about something feminine or non-human (in this case, the scales)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added مِ prefix and ي after first root letter to create noun pattern for tools/instruments
Added ين at the end to make the word plural (more than one scale)
Added damma to show this is the subject of خَفَّتْ (became light)
Added هُۥ to show possession

steps.m: to lead or be in front

steps.n: The ُ (damma) changes the word into a noun meaning 'mother' or 'source/origin'
Added فَ at the start to show this follows from the previous statement
Added هُ with the prolongation mark ۥ to show possession (his). This word is the subject being described by هَاوِيَةٌ (abyss/pit).
Added ا after first letter and ِ after second letter to show this describes something that does the falling. This word describes what أم (mother) is like in this ayah.
Added ة to make the word feminine since it describes أم (mother)
Added tanween (double damma) to show this is indefinite - meaning 'a' falling thing rather than 'the' falling thing
This is a question word used to ask about things or situations (like 'what' in English)
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it
Extended the final alif to add emphasis to the question. This is common in questions in the Quran.
Basic meaning of knowing or understanding something
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb
Adding أَ at the beginning and sukun on د makes the verb causative - meaning to make someone know something
Added the alif maksura (ىٰ) to show this is the final form of the weak letter
The كَ at the end means 'you' - showing who is being made to know. This connects to the question word مَا (what) that comes after it.
This is a question word used to ask about something unknown. In this ayah, it's asking 'what is it?' about هِيَهْ (it)
Added fatha mark on م to show correct pronunciation
This is a pronoun used to refer to feminine singular subjects

steps.m: what is it? (expressing shock about جَهَنَّمَ)
steps.n: Added ه with sukun at the end to turn this into an exclamation of shock or horror. This is asking about Hell (جَهَنَّمَ) in a way that shows how terrible it is.
Connected the root letters ن-و-ر and added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to form the basic noun meaning 'light'
The middle letter و becomes ا, and two dots (tanween) are added at the end to mean 'a fire'
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed from verb (it became hot) to describing noun (something that is hot)
Added tanween (ٌ) to match the indefinite state of نَارٌ
